title: Cosmic Hum Auditory Sensitivity
definition: The ability of an individual, Elian Voss, to perceive the low-frequency vibrations in the universe known as the 'cosmic hum'.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A person named Elian can hear a faint, continuous sound that everyone else thinks is silent. It's like his leg has a tiny radio tuned to a station only he can hear.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Elian Voss possesses an unusual sensitivity to the low-frequency cosmic hum, detecting it through minute vibrations in his right femur. This ability is not typical human hearing.
difficulty_levels.expert: Elian's auditory sensitivity to the cosmic hum represents a unique biological anomaly, allowing him to perceive vibrations imperceptible to most humans. The phenomenon may provide insights into undiscovered properties of the universe or potential evolutionary adaptations.

extra_fields.insight: The notion that there are vibrations in the universe beyond human auditory perception.
